data.title: Life Time Group Holdings, Inc. will report Q3 2025 financial results on Nov 4, 2025; press release before market open.
data.description: CHANHASSEN, Minnesota - Life Time Group Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: LTH) has scheduled the release of its third quarter 2025 financial results for Tuesday, November 4, 2025, before the market opens. The Company also plans to hold a conference to discuss these results.

data.title: Quantum PKI names Joshua Kuebel as Chief Technology Officer, emphasizing commitment to security. (Words: 85)
data.description: In Chicago today, Quantum PKI, a leading provider of Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) solutions, revealed the promotion of Joshua Kuebel as its new Chief Technology Officer. With a remarkable track record in delivering technical prowess and strategic vision over the past year, Kuebel's appointment highlights the company's ongoing dedication to enhancing enterprise security and fostering digital trust. With more than 25 years of practical experience in the Information Technology and Security sectors, Joshua Kuebel's elevation signifies a significant step towards achieving the company's objectives.
US TREASURY INTERVENED IN ARGENTINA FX MARKETS ON THURSDAY
BESSENT: LOOK FORWARD TO THE MEETING BETWEEN PRESIDENT TRUMP AND PRESIDENT MILEI ON OCTOBER 14

data.title: US futures turned positive, with Dow Jones up 0.047%, S&P 500 up 0.022%, Nasdaq 100 up 0.099%. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.17%. Trilogy Metals surged 180.38% in premarket following a $35.6 million investment from the White House. Markets see a 92.5% likelihood of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates in October. Trump willing to work with Democrats after government shutdown. Impacts of the shutdown seen in delayed trade deficit data release.
data.description: US futures edged slightly above the flatline on Tuesday after a mixed market on Monday. The rally in AI-linked stocks, fueled by Advanced Micro Devices Inc.'s deal with OpenAI, continued to boost Wall Street. President Trump expressed willingness to collaborate with Democrats but insisted on government reopening first. The U.S. trade deficit data was delayed due to the ongoing government shutdown which began on Oct. 1. 10-year Treasury bond yield was at 4.17% and two-year bond at 3.59%. CME Group's FedWatch tool showed a 92.5% likelihood of Fed cutting interest rates in October. Futures for major indices were up: Dow Jones 0.047%, S&P 500 0.022%, Nasdaq 100 0.099%, Russell 2000 0.088%. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF rose in premarket trading. Trilogy Metals Inc. surged 180.38% as the White House announced a $35.6 million investment for mining exploration in Alaska's Ambler Mining District, with Benzinga's Edge Stock Rankings showing strong price trends across the board for TMQ.

data.title: Tesla FSD v14 released, cheap Model Y anticipated. AppLovin rebounds after big losses on SEC probe. Dow Jones Futures update.
data.description: A limited number of users received the Tesla Full Self-Driving version 14 update, while there are rumors circulating about a potential budget-friendly release of the Model Y on Tuesday. AppLovin faced significant financial losses following an investigation by the Securities and Exchange Commission. Market indicators suggest a positive turn for AppLovin as Dow Jones Futures rise. The details were recently discussed in an article by Investor's Business Daily, which highlighted the potential launch of a cheaper Tesla Model Y and the current situation of AppLovin amidst regulatory scrutiny.

data.title: Soda Health rebrands as Evermore, an outcomes company, reflecting its role as the top smart benefits administration platform.
data.description: Evermore, formerly known as Soda Health, is now a prominent outcomes company specializing in benefits administration through its Smart Benefits platform. This rebranding marks a new direction for the company, which has been a pioneer in the field.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures up, Tesla stable with AI stocks rising. Jobs report halted by shutdown.
data.description: Stock futures are on the rise, with Tesla making efforts to stabilize. Broadcom is leading the surge in AI stocks, indicating strong buy signals. The release of the jobs report has been halted due to the current government shutdown. Dow Jones futures are up in response to these developments.

data.title: BLS data halted due to government shutdown. Chicago Fed predicts 4.34% unemployment for Sept; private sources wary of weak hiring.
data.description: The U.S. government shutdown has halted Bureau of Labor Statistics data releases, including the critical September jobs report. The Chicago Fed has stepped in with a forecast predicting a steady unemployment rate of 4.34% for September 2025 based on 11 job market indicators. Private data sources indicate a weak labor market with low holiday hiring plans that could impact payrolls through year-end. The forecast shows layoffs at 2.10%, hiring rates for unemployed workers at 45.22%, and a 28.2% probability of no change in the BLS-reported rate. Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee emphasized the reliance on real-time data during the shutdown.

data.title: Dow hits record amidst shutdown risk; Nvidia stock breaks out. CoreWeave and Nvidia are winners.
data.description: Today, the stock market saw a slight increase in indexes, with the Dow reaching a record high despite potential shutdown risks. CoreWeave and Nvidia were notable winners in the market. Nvidia in particular broke out significantly. This positive trend was covered in live updates by Investor's Business Daily. The market's performance reflects investors' optimism, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average and other key indexes showing promising gains. The latest data indicates a favorable outlook for CoreWeave and Nvidia stocks, offering potential opportunities for investors to capitalize on the market's upward momentum.
Brent crude futures settle at $65.22/bbl, down $1.03, 1.55%.
The two explosions in Kabul resulted from an airstrike targeting a car in the center of the Afghan capital — reports

data.title: Ivory Prize Summit at University of Utah on Oct 29 to honor housing innovators; features Utah Gov. Spencer Cox.
data.description: Utah Governor Spencer Cox is scheduled to give a speech during a livestreamed summit on October 9, 2025 in Salt Lake City. The event will showcase the Ivory Prize winners for their creative solutions in addressing housing affordability. Ivory Innovations, a non-profit academic center affiliated with the University of Utah David Eccles, is organizing the summit. This year’s Ivory Prize winners will be recognized for their innovative approaches. It is shaping up to be a significant event highlighting groundbreaking initiatives in tackling housing challenges.
Indonesia prevents Israeli participation in the World Gymnastics Championship - reports
